question: What’s the default worker thread count, and where does Goose actually use it when building worker opts?
reference_answer: 5 threads default; consumed in worker defaults. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:6; src/goose/worker.clj:42-44

question: What’s the default graceful shutdown time, and how does Redis shutdown differ from RabbitMQ shutdown?
reference_answer: 30 sec default; Redis waits `awaitTermination` and extends heartbeat expiry; RMQ polls active count via trampoline. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:7; src/goose/brokers/redis/worker.clj:24,38; src/goose/brokers/redis/heartbeat.clj:31; src/goose/brokers/rmq/worker.clj:17-21,38

question: What comes in `retry/default-opts` out of the box (`max-retries`, delay fn, `skip-dead-queue`, etc.)?
reference_answer: Retry defaults map: max-retries 27; delay fn `default-retry-delay-sec`; retry-queue nil; handlers default; skip-dead-queue false. refs: src/goose/retry.clj:34-66

question: If `retry-count` is 3, what does the `retry-count^4` part contribute to default backoff (ignoring jitter)?
reference_answer: `retry-count^4` term at count=3 is 81 seconds component (plus fixed 20 and jitter term). refs: src/goose/retry.clj:22-27

question: What are the default Redis scheduler knobs (poll interval + pop limits), and where are they used?
reference_answer: Defaults: polling 5s; scheduled pop 50; cron pop 50; used in scheduler loop and due-fetch queries. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:48-50; src/goose/brokers/redis/scheduler.clj:34-47; src/goose/brokers/redis/commands.clj:227; src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:65

question: What’s the max RMQ delay Goose allows, and what error do I get if I go past it?
reference_answer: RMQ delay cap is 4294967295 ms; scheduling beyond throws `MAX_DELAY limit breached...`. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:67; src/goose/brokers/rmq/commands.clj:95-96; test/goose/brokers/rmq/integration_test.clj:97-100

question: What are the default sync publisher-confirm settings in RMQ, and how do retries work there?
reference_answer: Sync confirm defaults: timeout 1000ms, max-retries 3, retry-delay 100ms; `sync-enqueue` wraps publish+confirm in retry loop. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/publisher_confirms.clj:20-24; src/goose/brokers/rmq/commands.clj:42-55

question: What are the default StatsD settings, and what extra setup call only happens when metrics are enabled?
reference_answer: StatsD defaults: enabled true, localhost:8125, prefix goose., sample-rate 1.0, tags {}; setup called only when enabled. refs: src/goose/metrics/statsd.clj:29-50,65-68

question: Which queue names are protected, and how does spec validation stop me from using them directly?
reference_answer: Protected queues are scheduled/dead/cron/cron-entries; `::queue` spec rejects protected and already-prefixed names. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:17-22; src/goose/specs.clj:97-100

question: What Redis key prefixes does Goose use (queue, in-progress, process, heartbeat, batch), and who uses which?
reference_answer: Prefixes: queue/in-progress/process/heartbeat/batch; used by worker process-set/in-progress queue and heartbeat key construction. refs: src/goose/defaults.clj:10-14; src/goose/brokers/redis/worker.clj:76-78; src/goose/brokers/redis/heartbeat.clj:7-8

question: Why does Goose make me inject broker state instead of deriving it from config each call?
reference_answer: Design decision: broker state must be injected (DI), not memoized from config by Goose internals. refs: architecture-decisions/pages/interface.md:17,20

question: Why doesn’t Goose run a separate console server, and what function should my app mount instead?
reference_answer: No dedicated console server; app should embed `console/app-handler` in its Ring app. refs: architecture-decisions/pages/console.md:22,50; src/goose/console.clj:265-283

question: In RMQ mode, which Broker protocol methods are implemented, and which ones are just not there?
reference_answer: RabbitMQ record implements enqueue/schedule/start-worker + limited enqueued/dead APIs + handler; many protocol APIs declared but absent. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/broker.clj:24-67; src/goose/broker.clj:29-67

question: Why does Goose avoid RMQ DLQ/TTL retry flow, and what queue-head TTL gotcha is called out?
reference_answer: ADR says TTL/DLQ retry flow is avoided due queue-head expiry blocking shorter TTL messages behind longer TTL ones. refs: architecture-decisions/pages/error-handling.md:24-29

question: Can you walk me through `perform-async` from API call to actually applying the target function?
reference_answer: Flow: client enqueue builds job then broker enqueue; worker consumes and `apply` executes resolved fn symbol. refs: src/goose/client.clj:49-58,60-80; src/goose/consumer.clj:5-7; src/goose/utils.clj:72; src/goose/brokers/redis/consumer.clj:19-21

question: For Redis, what path does `perform-in-sec` take from timestamp calc to getting into the ready queue?
reference_answer: `perform-in-sec` uses `sec+current-epoch-ms`; Redis scheduler stores in sorted-set, poll loop moves due jobs to ready queue. refs: src/goose/client.clj:129; src/goose/utils.clj:55; src/goose/brokers/redis/scheduler.clj:10-19,22-47

question: If a job is scheduled in the past, what does Redis do vs RabbitMQ, and which side of the queue gets used?
reference_answer: Past schedule: both adapters enqueue to front/high-priority path immediately; future schedules use delayed storage/plugin. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/scheduler.clj:14-17; src/goose/brokers/rmq/scheduler.clj:10-12

question: What’s the exact Redis wrapper order around job execution, and why does that order matter?
reference_answer: Redis wrapper order: middlewares -> metrics -> latency -> batch state update -> retry wrapper. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/worker.clj:46-55

question: What’s the exact RMQ wrapper order, and when does ACK happen relative to retry/dead handling?
reference_answer: RMQ wrapper order: middlewares -> metrics -> latency -> retry -> recovery/ACK; ACK happens after downstream call succeeds. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/worker.clj:46-55; src/goose/brokers/rmq/consumer.clj:11-20

question: How does Goose decide whether to record execution vs scheduled vs cron vs retry latency?
reference_answer: Latency type chosen by job fields priority: retry-at, schedule-run-at, cron-run-at, else enqueued-at execution latency. refs: src/goose/job.clj:45-55; src/goose/metrics.clj:59-69

question: When a Redis job fails, how is `:state` updated before Goose decides retry vs dead?
reference_answer: On failure, state gets error message + first/last failed timestamps + retry-count increment, then retry/dead routing executes. refs: src/goose/retry.clj:80-93; src/goose/brokers/redis/retry.clj:36-45

question: In Goose, what does `retry-count` really mean, and how many total executions happen for `max-retries` 0 vs 5?
reference_answer: `retry-count` counts retries not executions; total max executions = max-retries + 1 (explicit comment). refs: src/goose/retry.clj:86-91

question: Do Redis and RMQ retry boundary checks behave the same, or is there an edge-case mismatch?
reference_answer: Redis uses `>=` via helper; RMQ uses `<` branch to retry else bury; boundary behavior is equivalent. refs: src/goose/retry.clj:29-32; src/goose/brokers/rmq/retry.clj:45-48

question: If `skip-dead-queue` is true, which handlers/state updates still happen anyway?
reference_answer: Even with `skip-dead-queue=true`, death handler still runs and `:died-at` is set; only dead queue insertion is skipped. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/retry.clj:24-34; src/goose/brokers/rmq/retry.clj:24-36

question: How does `retry-queue` turn into `ready-retry-queue`, and when does Goose route to it?
reference_answer: Retry queue is prefixed into `:ready-retry-queue`; runtime routing picks retry queue when job has failure state. refs: src/goose/retry.clj:68-76; src/goose/job.clj:21-25

question: What are the batch states, and exactly how are they computed from job counts?
reference_answer: Batch states: in-progress/success/dead/partial-success from enqueued+retrying+success+dead counts. refs: src/goose/batch.clj:31-45

question: How does Redis batch handling avoid enqueueing the callback more than once under concurrency?
reference_answer: Concurrency safety: batch set transition + count read happen atomically in one transaction; only terminal observer enqueues callback. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/batch.clj:118-134,159-160

question: What keys does Redis create for a batch, and what does each one hold?
reference_answer: Batch keys: hash + 4 sets; enqueue writes hash/set and pushes jobs. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/batch.clj:14-19,23-31

question: At batch completion, which keys get `EXPIRE NX` and why those specific ones?
reference_answer: On completion: callback enqueued, status set, hash/success/dead keys expired with NX; enqueued/retrying sets are consumed by moves. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/batch.clj:73-91

question: What exactly does `batch/delete` remove for enqueued vs retrying jobs in Redis?
reference_answer: `batch/delete`: delete enqueued jobs from ready queue; retrying jobs from scheduled set or retry queue; then delete batch keys. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/api/batch.clj:11-38

question: What fields are stored in a cron entry, and what timezone is used if I don’t pass one?
reference_answer: Cron entry stores name/schedule/timezone/job-description; timezone defaults to system ZoneId when omitted. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:13-21,15

question: Why is cron registration effectively idempotent for the same `cron-name`, and what gets updated transactionally?
reference_answer: Idempotent register overwrites by name inside transaction updating hash and sorted-set due time. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:38-49

question: How are due cron entries fetched per tick, and which default limits that batch size?
reference_answer: Due cron fetch uses zrangebyscore with limit from default `redis-cron-names-pop-limit`. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:61-65; src/goose/defaults.clj:50

question: When cron entries are enqueued, how is `cron-run-at` computed and where is cron parsing done?
reference_answer: Generated cron job gets `:cron-run-at` from previous execution timestamp; parsing done in cron parsing namespace. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:84-87; src/goose/cron/parsing.clj:33-39

question: What cron limitations are called out in ADR (minimum frequency and backfill behavior)?
reference_answer: ADR limitations: minimum frequency one minute; backfill only one missed cron run. refs: architecture-decisions/pages/cron-jobs.md:20-21

question: Why does Goose store both `queue` and `ready-queue` in the job payload?
reference_answer: Both `:queue` and `:ready-queue` are stored so API queries can use user queue while internal queue stays explicit. refs: src/goose/job.clj:10-15,33-36

question: How does console param validation deal with bad UUIDs, bad filter types, and invalid page/limit numbers?
reference_answer: Validation uses `validate-or-default` + `str->long` + page-level specs; invalid values fall back/empty instead of hard failure. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/console/specs.clj:24-49; src/goose/brokers/redis/console/pages/enqueued.clj:90-110

question: What filter types are allowed on enqueued/scheduled/dead pages, and how do value validations differ?
reference_answer: Allowed filter types differ by page: enqueued(id/execute-fn-sym/type), dead(id/execute-fn-sym/queue), scheduled(id/execute-fn-sym/type/queue). refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/console/specs.clj:9-13; src/goose/brokers/redis/console/pages/enqueued.clj:96-104; src/goose/brokers/redis/console/pages/dead.clj:19-27; src/goose/brokers/redis/console/pages/scheduled.clj:90-103

question: How does `app-handler` support HTML method override, and which request param actually flips the method?
reference_answer: `wrap-method-override` reads `:params :_method` and replaces request method; behavior covered in console tests. refs: src/goose/console.clj:239-245; test/goose/console_test.clj:25-30

question: Why does Redis `prioritise-execution` return that nested transaction-like shape instead of just true/false?
reference_answer: Return shape mirrors Carmine transaction pipeline result (`[["OK" ...] [lrem rpush]]`) by design for consistency. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/commands.clj:145-161; src/goose/brokers/redis/api/enqueued_jobs.clj:34-40

question: How does `get-by-range` ordering differ for scheduled jobs vs dead jobs in Redis?
reference_answer: Scheduled range is ascending score; dead range uses reverse score order. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/api/scheduled_jobs.clj:43-45; src/goose/brokers/redis/api/dead_jobs.clj:59-61; test/goose/brokers/redis/api_test.clj:178,278

question: Why does RMQ console pre-declare the dead queue before rendering, and what failure is it avoiding?
reference_answer: RMQ console pre-declares dead queue to avoid RabbitMQ errors when querying/purging dead queue before first publish. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/console.clj:23-33,72-73

question: What does `relative-time` return for past vs future timestamps (including the wording)?
reference_answer: `relative-time` prefixes future with `in` and past with `overdue by`; granularity day/hour/min/sec. refs: src/goose/utils.clj:117-135

question: How does orphan recovery in Redis decide a worker is dead and replay its preserved jobs?
reference_answer: Orphan recovery scans process set, checks heartbeat key liveness, replays preservation queue jobs, then removes dead process id. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/orphan_checker.clj:17-24; src/goose/brokers/redis/heartbeat.clj:7-11

question: When there’s no due work, how is sleep time computed in scheduler/metrics/orphan loops?
reference_answer: If no due work: scheduler sleeps using global workers; metrics also global; orphan checker sleeps by local workers. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/scheduler.clj:39-47; src/goose/brokers/redis/metrics.clj:55-58; src/goose/brokers/redis/orphan_checker.clj:34-40

question: What exact scenario throws `MAX_DELAY limit breached`, and through which public API do I hit it?
reference_answer: `MAX_DELAY` exception thrown in RMQ schedule command; surfaced by `perform-in-sec` integration test for huge delay. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/commands.clj:96; test/goose/brokers/rmq/integration_test.clj:97-100

question: When does `random-element` throw `List is empty`, and which RMQ paths depend on it?
reference_answer: `random-element` throws when passed empty list; RMQ broker/console repeatedly rely on random channel selection. refs: src/goose/utils.clj:93-97; src/goose/brokers/rmq/broker.clj:29,36,47; src/goose/brokers/rmq/console.clj:25,33

question: What input/state leads to `CHANNEL_MAX limit reached` in `rmq.channel/open`?
reference_answer: Throw path is explicit in RMQ channel open when `lch/open` returns nil (channel max exhausted). refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/channel.clj:14-26

question: Which `perform-batch` input shapes fail specs, and how is callback arity checked?
reference_answer: Batch spec requires args as seq of seqs and callback fn arity 2; invalid shapes/callbacks are asserted in specs tests. refs: src/goose/specs.clj:112-116,224; test/goose/specs_test.clj:73-81

question: Which bad `console-opts` combinations are expected to fail in tests?
reference_answer: Malformed console opts tested: missing/invalid broker, app-name, route-prefix; console opts spec requires all three keys with string types. refs: test/goose/specs_test.clj:154-159; src/goose/specs.clj:188-192,230

question: In `scheduled-jobs/prioritises-execution`, is there a bug where original jobs are passed instead of only validated ones?
reference_answer: Yes, likely bug: `prioritises-execution` computes valid subset but passes original `jobs` to move call; contrast dead replay uses filtered list. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/api/scheduled_jobs.clj:26-34; src/goose/brokers/redis/api/dead_jobs.clj:31-40

question: In Redis `batch/get-batch`, is there a key-name mismatch for retrying-set lookup, and what does that break?
reference_answer: Likely bug: `get-batch` destructures `retrying-job-set` but `batch-keys` returns `retrying-set`, so retrying count can be wrong/nil path. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/batch.clj:14-19,45-52

question: From broker protocol + RMQ record only, which `goose.api` surfaces are unsupported in RMQ mode?
reference_answer: Unsupported surface comes from protocol-method mismatch: Broker declares many methods but RMQ record implements only subset. refs: src/goose/broker.clj:29-67; src/goose/brokers/rmq/broker.clj:46-63

question: Can you trace `perform-in-sec` on Redis end-to-end, including final queue placement via `ready-or-retry-queue`?
reference_answer: End-to-end: `perform-in-sec` -> epoch calc -> `enqueue` -> Redis `run-at` sorted-set/front queue -> scheduler poll -> move due jobs by `ready-or-retry-queue`. refs: src/goose/client.clj:49-58,129; src/goose/brokers/redis/scheduler.clj:10-19,22-31,39-47; src/goose/job.clj:21-25

question: Are Redis and RMQ retry terminal conditions equivalent for edge values?
reference_answer: Semantics align: both allow up to `max-retries` retries after initial failure; total executions `max-retries + 1`. refs: src/goose/retry.clj:29-32,86-91; src/goose/brokers/rmq/retry.clj:45-48

question: How do wrapper orders differ between Redis and RMQ workers, and what behavior differences does that cause?
reference_answer: Wrapper order differs only in RMQ ACK stage; Redis has batch-state wrapper, RMQ has ACK/recovery wrapper. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/worker.clj:46-55; src/goose/brokers/rmq/worker.clj:46-55; src/goose/brokers/rmq/consumer.clj:11-20

question: During Redis shutdown, when is the process-set entry removed immediately vs left for orphan checker?
reference_answer: On stop: heartbeat key removed; process removed only if in-progress queue empty, else orphan checker later recovers and removes. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/heartbeat.clj:37-44; src/goose/brokers/redis/worker.clj:40; src/goose/brokers/redis/orphan_checker.clj:17-24

question: How do cron register/due-fetch/enqueue-reschedule steps work together in Redis, and why is `ensure-sequential` needed?
reference_answer: Cron coordination: register writes hash+zset due time; due fetch reads zset then hget; enqueue loop pushes jobs and reschedules; `ensure-sequential` handles wcar single-item shape. refs: src/goose/brokers/redis/cron.clj:38-49,61-80,89-103

question: In RMQ async confirms, what race does `locking ch` prevent, and what metadata comes back for correlation?
reference_answer: `locking ch` prevents seqNo/publish race on shared channel; async enqueue returns `{id channel-number delivery-tag}` for correlation. refs: src/goose/brokers/rmq/commands.clj:32-39; test/goose/brokers/rmq/integration_test.clj:133-140

question: Which validations only fail when specs are instrumented, vs ones always enforced by manual asserts?
reference_answer: Frequently-called functions rely on `specs/instrument`; single-use constructors/start use manual `assert-*` checks. refs: src/goose/specs.clj:241-253,260,267-307; test/goose/specs_test.clj:42-46
